---
title: "The Cases"
---

<!-- DO NOT EDIT. Generated by scripts/build-book.R from the Markdown
     in case-studies/. Edit the source case files and re-run the build. -->


Six crimes against causality await. Each one is a real-looking study, chart,
or algorithm with a fatal flaw buried inside it. Read the crime scene, work
through the interrogation, and form your own verdict before you reveal the
solution.

Cases are investigated by one of two detectives — see [The Detectives](../intro/detectives.qmd).
Difficulty runs from ● (Easy) to ●●● (Hard).

| # | Case | Detective | Difficulty | Topics | Fallacies |
|---|------|-----------|:----------:|--------|-----------|
| 01 | [**Death by Beats**](case-01.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>A viral chart claims rap and hip-hop musicians die at around 30. The trend looks deadly — but is it real?</span> | Wilbert Wright | ●●○<br>Medium | Public health, Data visualization, Survival analysis | Right censoring, Survivorship bias |
| 02 | [**The Hospital Hit List**](case-02.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>A newspaper ranks hospitals by survival rate and names the deadliest. But every condition tells the opposite story.</span> | Wilbert Wright | ●●●<br>Hard | Public health, Hospital quality, Aggregation | Simpson's paradox, Confounding |
| 03 | [**The Prophet Algorithm**](case-03.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>An inspection algorithm boasts 97% accuracy. Strip away the base rate and most of its flags are wrong.</span> | Nora Nightingale | ●●○<br>Medium | Machine learning, Public safety, AI governance | Base rate neglect, Accuracy paradox |
| 04 | [**The Watchful Eye**](case-04.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>A facial-recognition system is 99.9% accurate — and still wrong nearly every time it raises an alarm.</span> | Nora Nightingale | ●●●<br>Hard | Machine learning, Surveillance, Algorithmic fairness | Base rate neglect, False positive paradox |
| 05 | [**A Glass a Day**](case-05.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>A survey suggests moderate drinking lowers mortality. The catch is hiding in who counts as a non-drinker.</span> | Wilbert Wright | ●●○<br>Medium | Public health, Nutrition, Epidemiology | Reference group bias, Confounding |
| 12 | [**Peaking Too Early**](case-12.qmd)<br><span class='case-blurb'>A headline says childhood prodigies rarely become adult stars. The reverse claim it implies does not follow.</span> | Nora Nightingale | ●●●<br>Hard | Human performance, Talent, Probability | Confusion of the inverse, Conditional probability |
